use_codes.desc: TREATMENT OF DIABETES WITH AN AMYLIN AGONIST
TREATMENT OF DIABETES WITH AN AMYLIN AGONIST, INCLUDING WITH INSULIN
TREATMENT OF A MAMMAL HAVING A NEED OF OR REDUCED ABILITY TO PRODUCE INSULIN WITH AN INSULIN AND AN AMYLIN SUCH AS PRAMLINTIDE
USE OF AN AMYLIN AGONIST TO REDUCE GASTRIC MOTILITY AND TREAT POST PRANDIAL HYPERGLYCEMIA
USE OF AN AMYLIN AGONIST HAVING SPECIFIED BINDING ACTIVITY TO REDUCE GASTRIC MOTILITY, INCLUDING USE THROUGH PARENTERAL ADMINISTRATION

indications.text:
SYMLIN is indicated as an adjunctive treatment in patients with type 1 or type 2 diabetes who
use mealtime insulin therapy and who have failed to achieve desired glucose control despite
optimal insulin therapy.

use mealtime insulin therapy and who have failed to achieve desired glucose control despite
optimal insulin therapy.
